title: CRISPR/Cas9-based knock-out in human primary T cells
author: Bulent Arman Aksoy
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.quxdwxn

steps.title: RNase Zap everything before you start
steps.instruction: RNase Zap everything before you start

steps.title: Resuspend the crRNA (2 nmol) in...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the crRNA (2 nmol) in 100 ul (making 20 uM) and tracRNA (20 nmol) in 1 ml RNA storage buffer (making 20 uM). Aliquot and keep at -80°C.

steps.title: Need 0
steps.instruction: Need 0.375ul crRNA and 0.375 ul tracRNA per well (this way, we’ll use 7.5pmol sgRNA per 200K cells as Neon protocol suggests).

steps.title: Mix 1
steps.instruction: Mix 1.5 ul crRNA and 1.5 ul tracRNA (for 4 rxns) in a PCR tube.

steps.title: For Cas9, Neon suggests 1250 ng...
steps.instruction: For Cas9, Neon suggests 1250 ng Cas9 protein per 200K cells. Our Cas9 is at 5 mg/ml concentration. So, we will need 0.25 ul Cas9/200K cells.

steps.title: After incubation of the sgRNA, slowly...
steps.instruction: After incubation of the sgRNA, slowly add 1ul Cas9 (for 4 reactions) in the PCR tube, mix and incubate at 37°C for 15 mins. 00:15:00

steps.title: Need 200K cells for one electroporation event
steps.instruction: Need 200K cells for one electroporation event. The cells should be in 9 ul T buffer (9 ul T buffer reaction), so that the total volume (9 ul cells + 1 ul RNP mix) will be 10ul for the Neon 10 tip.

steps.title: Debead and count the activated cells
steps.instruction: Debead and count the activated cells.

steps.title: We need 200,000*3*24 (14
steps.instruction: We need 200,000*3*24 (14.4 million cells).

steps.title: To be safe, assume 4 reactions...
steps.instruction: To be safe, assume 4 reactions well so we will need 200,000*4*24 (19 million) cells.

steps.title: 19 million cells are actually good for 96 wells
steps.instruction: 19 million cells are actually good for 96 wells.

steps.title: So, we need 96*9 = 864 ul T buffer
steps.instruction: So, we need 96*9 = 864 ul T buffer

steps.title: After debeading and counting the cells,...
steps.instruction: After debeading and counting the cells, spin them down at 200 x g for 7 mins.

steps.title: Aspirate the media as much as possible
steps.instruction: Aspirate the media as much as possible.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 864 ul T buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 864 ul T buffer.

steps.title: Add 36 ul of cell mix...
steps.instruction: Add 36 ul of cell mix to each PCR tube and mix well.

steps.title: The Cas9 RNP and cells are ready for electroporation
steps.instruction: The Cas9 RNP and cells are ready for electroporation

steps.title: Electroporation at 1600 V 10 ms...
steps.instruction: Electroporation at 1600 V 10 ms 3 pulses (Thermo Fisher Scientific Neon™ Transfection System, MPK5000S)

steps.title: Seed the electroporated cells on the...
steps.instruction: Seed the electroporated cells on the prepared 24-well-plate with warm T cell media.

steps.title: The time to profile CRISPR/Cas9 treated...
steps.instruction: The time to profile CRISPR/Cas9 treated cell depends on the particular assay of interest but in general, cells can be profiled via flow cytometer for surface proteins and via Western Blot for internal proteins 3 days after electroporation.

steps.title: Keep the RNA mix at 95°C for 5 minutes 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Keep the RNA mix at 95°C for 5 minutes 00:05:00

steps.title: Then keep the RNA mix at...
steps.instruction: Then keep the RNA mix at at 37°C for 25 mins. 00:25:00
